factors.summary: Overall demand appears stable with some growth in certain segments.

evidence.text: Organic sales for the three months ended March 31, 2023 increased by 4% compared with the same period of 2022. The organic increase was primarily driven by our Fire & Security segment due to price improvements and volume growth in each region. In addition, improved global end-markets in our Commercial HVAC business further benefited our results.

factors.summary: Order backlog is not explicitly discussed, but the company mentions challenges with supply chain constraints affecting sales.

evidence.text: Our operations are classified into three segments: HVAC, Refrigeration and Fire & Security. Our worldwide operations are affected by global and regional industrial, economic and political factors and trends. These include the mega-trends of urbanization, climate change and increasing requirements for food safety driven by the food needs of the growing global population and the rising standards of living in emerging markets.

factors.summary: The company has been successful in raising prices to mitigate inflationary pressures.

evidence.text: We continue to take pricing actions to mitigate supply chain and inflationary pressures, develop new products and services in order to remain competitive in our markets and use risk management strategies to mitigate various exposures.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments, particularly in the Fire & Security segment, which saw a significant increase in operating profit margin.

evidence.text: The Fire & Security segment provided a wide range of residential, commercial and industrial technologies designed to help protect people and property. For the three months ended March 31, 2023, Net sales in our Fire & Security segment were $869 million, a 6% increase compared with the same period of 2022.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, particularly in the Fire & Security segment, with a 9% organic increase in Net sales.

evidence.text: Organic Net sales decreased 5% compared to the prior year as each of the segment's businesses experienced challenges in certain end-markets during the period.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing in certain regions and segments, particularly in the Fire & Security segment, which saw volume growth in Europe and Asia.

evidence.text: The organic increase in Net sales of 9% was primarily driven by pricing improvements and volume growth compared with the prior year. Sales grew in all three regions with strong commercial results in Europe and Asia as current economic conditions and end-market demand improved.

factors.summary: The Fire & Security segment is experiencing the fastest growth, driven by strong demand in Europe and Asia.

evidence.text: The Fire & Security segment provided a wide range of residential, commercial and industrial technologies designed to help protect people and property. For the three months ended March 31, 2023, Net sales in our Fire & Security segment were $869 million, a 6% increase compared with the same period of 2022.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to inflationary pressures, but the company is taking steps to mitigate these through pricing and productivity initiatives.

evidence.text: Inflationary cost pressures have begun to moderate, but remain elevated and continue to affect our results. We expect that these challenges will continue to impact our businesses for the foreseeable future.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, but there is an increase in compensation and other employee-related costs.

evidence.text: For the three months ended March 31, 2023, Selling, general and administrative expenses were $721 million, a 20% increase compared with the same period of 2022. The increase is primarily due to the incremental selling, general and administrative expenses associated with TCC since the date of acquisition. In addition, higher compensation and other employee-related costs further contributed to the increase.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are under pressure due to higher input costs and supply chain challenges, but pricing improvements are helping to mitigate some of these impacts.

evidence.text: Gross margin as a percentage of Net sales decreased by 170 basis points compared with the same period of 2022. The decrease was primarily driven by volume reductions in certain end-markets compared with the prior year.

factors.summary: The company is taking proactive steps to limit the impact of supply chain challenges through cost savings initiatives and supply chain investments.

evidence.text: We continue to take proactive steps to limit the impact of these challenges and are working closely with our suppliers to ensure availability of products and implement other cost savings initiatives. In addition, we continue to invest in our supply chain to improve its resilience with a focus on automation, dual sourcing of critical components and localized manufacturing when feasible.

factors.summary: Productivity initiatives are contributing to margin improvements, particularly in the HVAC segment.

evidence.text: The main driver of the increase related to ongoing customer demand, pricing improvements and our continued focus on productivity initiatives. In addition, incremental results associated with TCC since the date of acquisition further benefited gross margin during the period.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its market share, particularly in the HVAC segment, which saw a 22% increase in Net sales.

evidence.text: The company is the leading global provider of healthy, safe, sustainable and intelligent building and cold chain solutions with a focus on providing differentiated, digitally-enabled lifecycle solutions to our customers.

factors.summary: The competitive environment is intensifying, with the company investing in new products and services to maintain differentiation.

evidence.text: We continue to invest in our business, take pricing actions to mitigate supply chain and inflationary pressures, develop new products and services in order to remain competitive in our markets and use risk management strategies to mitigate various exposures.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include its industry-leading brands and track record of innovation, which are being leveraged to drive growth.

evidence.text: Coupled with our focus on growth, innovation and operational efficiency, we expect to drive long-term future growth and increased value for our shareowners.

factors.summary: Customer diversification is evident across regions, with strong growth in Europe and Asia.

evidence.text: The Fire & Security segment provided a wide range of residential, commercial and industrial technologies designed to help protect people and property. For the three months ended March 31, 2023, Net sales in our Fire & Security segment were $869 million, a 6% increase compared with the same period of 2022.

factors.summary: Management has not provided specific forward guidance, but the tone suggests cautious optimism due to ongoing challenges.

evidence.text: There have been no significant changes in our critical accounting estimates.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious due to ongoing supply chain challenges and inflationary pressures.

evidence.text: We continue to take proactive steps to limit the impact of these challenges and are working closely with our suppliers to ensure availability of products and implement other cost savings initiatives.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include continued supply chain disruptions and elevated inflationary pressures.

evidence.text: These challenges will continue to impact our businesses for the foreseeable future. We expect that these challenges will continue to impact our businesses for the foreseeable future.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on growth, innovation, and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: Coupled with our focus on growth, innovation and operational efficiency, we expect to drive long-term future growth and increased value for our shareowners.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include commodity prices, currency fluctuations, and supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: These include the mega-trends of urbanization, climate change and increasing requirements for food safety driven by the food needs of the growing global population and the rising standards of living in emerging markets.

factors.summary: The integration of TCC is a near-term catalyst for growth in the HVAC segment.

evidence.text: The results of TCC's operations are included in our consolidated results since the acquisition date of August 1, 2022. Prior to the acquisition, we accounted for our minority ownership in TCC under the equity method of accounting and recognized our portion of earnings within Equity method investment in net earnings as part of operating expenses. As a result, prior period results may not be comparable to the current period.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2023 Compared with the Three Months Ended March 31, 2022

factors.summary: R&D spending is increasing, driven by investments in new product development and digital controls technologies.

evidence.text: Research and development costs relate to new product development and new technology innovation. Due to the variable nature of program development schedules, year-over-year spending levels can fluctuate. In addition, we continue to invest to prepare for future energy efficiency and refrigerant regulation changes and in digital controls technologies.

factors.summary: The company is investing in digital technologies and automation to improve supply chain resilience and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: In addition, we continue to invest in our supply chain to improve its resilience with a focus on automation, dual sourcing of critical components and localized manufacturing when feasible.

factors.summary: New products, such as those from the TCC acquisition, are contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: The acquisition included all of TCC's advanced research and development centers and global manufacturing operations, product pipeline and the long-term use of Toshiba's iconic brand. The acquisition was completed on August 1, 2022.

factors.summary: The supply chain is still disrupted, with challenges in component availability and transportation delays.

evidence.text: We continue to take proactive steps to limit the impact of these challenges and are working closely with our suppliers to ensure availability of products and implement other cost savings initiatives. In addition, we continue to invest in our supply chain to improve its resilience with a focus on automation, dual sourcing of critical components and localized manufacturing when feasible.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is not explicitly discussed, but the company is investing in localized manufacturing to improve resilience.

evidence.text: In addition, we continue to invest in our supply chain to improve its resilience with a focus on automation, dual sourcing of critical components and localized manufacturing when feasible.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are being managed through localized manufacturing and dual sourcing, but there is no explicit mention of inventory levels.

evidence.text: In addition, we continue to invest in our supply chain to improve its resilience with a focus on automation, dual sourcing of critical components and localized manufacturing when feasible.

factors.summary: Product quality and safety are not explicitly mentioned, but the company focuses on sustainable and intelligent solutions, implying a commitment to quality.

evidence.text: Carrier Global Corporation ('we' or 'our') is the leading global provider of healthy, safe, sustainable and intelligent building and cold chain solutions with a focus on providing differentiated, digitally-enabled lifecycle solutions to our customers.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are a headwind due to ongoing challenges from the COVID-19 pandemic and inflationary pressures.

evidence.text: The ongoing global economic recovery from the COVID-19 pandemic has caused significant challenges for global supply chains resulting in inflationary cost pressures, component shortages and transportation delays.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are impacting results, with a 2% negative impact on organic sales.

evidence.text: Organic sales for the three months ended March 31, 2023 increased by 4% compared with the same period of 2022. The components of the year-over-year change were as follows: Organic 4% Foreign currency translation (2%) Acquisitions and divestitures, net 11% Total % change 13%

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks are not explicitly mentioned, but the company operates in global markets and may be exposed to various risks.

evidence.text: Our worldwide operations are affected by global and regional industrial, economic and political factors and trends.

factors.summary: The company is increasing capital expenditures to improve supply chain resilience and invest in new technologies.

evidence.text: In addition, we continue to invest in our supply chain to improve its resilience with a focus on automation, dual sourcing of critical components and localized manufacturing when feasible.

factors.summary: The company is focused on sustainable and intelligent solutions, implying a commitment to ESG principles.

evidence.text: Carrier Global Corporation ('we' or 'our') is the leading global provider of healthy, safe, sustainable and intelligent building and cold chain solutions with a focus on providing differentiated, digitally-enabled lifecycle solutions to our customers.

factors.summary: Free cash flow experienced a decrease of $173 million in the first quarter of 2023 compared to the prior year period, while the sale of the Chubb Fire & Security business generated net proceeds of $2.9 billion, contributing positively to cash flow.

evidence.text: Net increase (decrease) in cash and cash equivalents and restricted cash $ (173) $ 597
On January 3, 2022, we completed the sale of Chubb (the 'Chubb Sale') for net proceeds of $2.9 billion and recognized a gain on the sale of $1.1 billion during the year ended December 31, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Sale of Chubb Fire & Security Business

factors.summary: The company is actively returning value to shareholders through both share repurchases and dividend increases, indicating a positive approach to shareholder returns.

evidence.text: The company is maintaining its share repurchase program with $2.1 billion remaining as of March 31, 2023, and increased dividends in April 2023.

We paid dividends on common stock during the three months ended March 31, 2023, totaling $154 million. In April 2023, the Board of Directors declared a dividend of $0.185 per share of common stock payable on May 24, 2023 to shareowners of record at the close of business on May 5, 2023.
Coupled with our focus on growth, innovation and operational efficiency, we expect to drive long-term future growth and increased value for our shareowners.

factors.summary: The company is actively engaged in mergers and acquisitions to enhance its product portfolio and support growth, as evidenced by the significant acquisition of Toshiba Carrier Corporation in the HVAC segment.

evidence.text: We continue to actively manage and strengthen our business portfolio to meet the current and future needs of our customers. This is accomplished through research and development activities with a focus on new product development and new technology innovation as well as sustaining activities with a focus on improving existing products and reducing production costs. We also pursue potential acquisitions to complement existing products and services to enhance our product portfolio. In addition, we routinely conduct discussions, evaluate targets and enter into agreements regarding possible acquisitions, divestitures, joint ventures and equity investments to manage our business portfolio.

On February 6, 2022, we entered into a binding agreement to acquire a majority ownership interest in Toshiba Carrier Corporation ('TCC'), a variable refrigerant flow ('VRF') and light commercial HVAC joint venture between Carrier and Toshiba Corporation. The acquisition was completed on August 1, 2022.

factors.summary: The company is actively working to reduce its debt, as evidenced by a net debt reduction of $181 million from December 2022 to March 2023. Additionally, the decrease in interest expense by 18% supports the notion of deleveraging or refinancing activities.

evidence.text: As of March 31, 2023, we had cash and cash equivalents of $3.3 billion, of which approximately 37% was held by our foreign subsidiaries. We manage our worldwide cash requirements by reviewing available funds and the cost effectiveness with which we can access funds held by foreign subsidiaries. On occasion, we are required to maintain cash deposits in connection with contractual obligations related to acquisitions, divestitures or other legal obligations. As of March 31, 2023 and December 31, 2022, the amount of such restricted cash was approximately $7 million and $39 million, respectively.
For the three months ended March 31, 2023, Interest expense was $71 million, a 18% decrease compared with the same period of 2022.

factors.summary: The impact of interest rate changes on the company appears mixed. While the company has effectively managed its debt and maintained strong credit ratings, leading to compliance with financial covenants, there has been a noticeable decrease in interest expense, indicating some positive effect from rate changes.

evidence.text: The Revolving Credit Facility, the Japanese Term Loan Facility and the indentures for the long-term notes contain affirmative and negative covenants customary for financings of these types, which among other things, limit our ability to incur additional liens, to make certain fundamental changes and to enter into sale and leaseback transactions. As of March 31, 2023, we were compliant with the covenants under the agreements governing our outstanding indebtedness.
For the three months ended March 31, 2023, Interest expense was $71 million, a 18% decrease compared with the same period of 2022.
